# CARE v0.2.0-alpha.3

A maintenance + capability release on top of v0.2.0-alpha.2. Adds a fifth optional PII provider — **OpenAI's privacy-filter** (Apache-2.0, opt-in) — closes a 17-item post-alpha.2 audit (offline-guard refactor leftovers, dependency hygiene, doc drift, latent governance-script bugs), and turns on a CI lint gate (`ruff`). No public API or CLI surface change.

> **Pre-1.0.** Core invariants — offline-first, fail-closed, plugin-based, no original PDFs in public exports — remain stable. The API and CLI surface may still change before 1.0.

---

## Which installer should I download?

Same lineup as v0.2.0-alpha.2. **For most users on a connected workstation, `CARE-0.2.0-core-online-Setup.exe` is the right choice.**

| File | When to use |
|---|---|
| `CARE-0.2.0-core-online-Setup.exe` | **Default.** Slim install (~120 MB). Connected workstation. WebView2 runtime is fetched automatically if missing. |
| `CARE-0.2.0-core-airgap-Setup.exe` | Same as above but bundles the WebView2 runtime offline (~150 MB extra). Pick this if your machine cannot reach Microsoft's update servers. |
| `CARE-0.2.0-ml-online-Setup.exe` | Adds `torch` + `transformers` (~2 GB) so optional Hugging Face plugins can run. **Model weights are not bundled** — install them separately, see below. |
| `CARE-0.2.0-ml-airgap-Setup.exe` | Same ML libraries with the offline WebView2 runtime. |
| `CARE-0.2.0-core.msi` | Enterprise SCCM / Intune deployment, slim. WebView2 is a documented prerequisite admins pre-stage. |
| `CARE-0.2.0-ml.msi` | SCCM / Intune deployment with the ML libraries. |

> **Asset names are unchanged across the v0.2.0 alpha series** — the file names embed the package version (`0.2.0`), not the tag suffix. If you have multiple alphas downloaded side-by-side, sort by date or use the SHA-256 table at the bottom of each release page to disambiguate.

---

## What's new since v0.2.0-alpha.2

### New optional PII provider: **OpenAI Privacy Filter**

CARE now ships a fifth PII provider plugin wrapping [`openai/privacy-filter`](https://huggingface.co/openai/privacy-filter), released by OpenAI on 2026-04-22 under **Apache-2.0** (commercial use allowed; no license-review-required flag). It's a 1.5B-parameter bidirectional token-classification model with sparse mixture-of-experts routing (50M active per token), trained for high-throughput PII detection and masking on local hardware.

**Disabled by default**, like every other optional provider. Operators enable it in `config.yaml` after dropping the model files into `models/pii/openai-privacy-filter/`. The provider:

- Loads only from a local `model_dir`. Refuses `allow_network=true` and `local_files_only=false` at load time.
- Re-applies the Hugging Face offline env vars on every load.
- Records per-file SHA-256 checksums on the model manifest for tamper detection.
- Reuses the existing `HFTokenClassificationMixin` lifecycle, so the new file is ~180 lines of label policy + manifest, not a fresh HF integration.

**Eight model labels** map to canonical CARE entity types (operator-overridable via `config.yaml` `label_map`):

| Privacy-filter label | care `ENTITY_TYPE` |
|---|---|
| `private_person` | `PERSON_NAME` |
| `private_address` | `ADDRESS` |
| `private_email` | `EMAIL` |
| `private_phone` | `PHONE_NUMBER` |
| `private_date` | `DATE_OF_BIRTH` |
| `account_number` | `INSURANCE_POLICY` |
| `secret` | `CASE_NUMBER` |
| `private_url` | dropped (opt-in via `label_map`) |

The provider needs `transformers>=5.6` (the version that added `OpenAIPrivacyFilterForTokenClassification`); the `[ml]` extra and the `ml` installer SKU bundle a compatible version. Total disk footprint is ~2.84 GB for the local checkpoint — see `models/pii/openai-privacy-filter/README.md` for the operator install + download checklist.

The Plugins page picks the new provider up automatically — no GUI changes were needed because `/api/plugins` is fully data-driven.

### Plugin architecture cleanup (post-alpha.2 audit)

A multi-agent audit after alpha.2 surfaced several refactor leftovers, latent bugs, and doc-drift items. All addressed in this release; each carries a regression test where applicable.

- **`assert_offline_config` is now centrally enforced.** PR #28 introduced a shared offline-mode config gate on the OCR/PII/DocumentAI base classes, but four providers (`presidio`, `kosmos25`, `layoutlm`, `hf_local`) kept their own inline checks and never migrated. They now call `self.assert_offline_config(config)` from `load()`, and a parametrized meta-test asserts every concrete provider's `load({"allow_network": True})` raises `ConfigError` — drift like the original is caught at PR time.
- **`HF_OFFLINE_ENV` is now force-pinned by every plugin.** Two providers used `os.environ.setdefault(...)` — which silently keeps a stale value if a misbehaving test or CLI invocation flipped `TRANSFORMERS_OFFLINE=0` mid-process. The new `apply_hf_offline_env()` helper force-overwrites every key on plugin load, exactly matching the safer semantics the `HFTokenClassificationMixin` had documented all along. The startup guard at `core.offline_guard.enable()` keeps `setdefault` semantics so it doesn't overwrite operator-set values at process start.
- **`scripts/governance_check.py` had latent dead checks.** Two of its policy gates (`check_no_public_export_of_originals`, `check_desktop_module_no_external_urls`) were silently no-op'ing because they pointed at four `backend/app/...` paths that never existed in this repo. The check now points at the real `care/` layout, so unsafe export markers and non-loopback URLs in the desktop wrapper are actually caught.
- **`LLMProvider` now exposes `assert_offline_config` for parity** with the OCR / PII / DocumentAI bases. Local-LLM providers (`hf_local`, future Ollama, etc.) call it from their own `load()`; cloud providers continue to do their own offline-mode rejection.
- **`HFTokenClassificationMixin._extract_label` handles BIOES.** RoBERTa-NER and Piiranha use the BIO scheme; OpenAI Privacy Filter uses BIOES. The shared label-stripper now strips `B-`/`I-`/`E-`/`S-` prefixes defensively, regardless of which scheme the underlying checkpoint emits or whether the HF pipeline aggregation already collapsed them.

### Documentation alignment

The `GOVERNANCE.md` repo-structure section, several `CONTRACT` references scattered across the codebase, and the `python -m care.cli ...` invocations in `docs/license-and-model-governance.md` were all stale relative to the actual layout and console-script entry points. Cleaned up:

- `GOVERNANCE.md` repository-structure block rewritten to reflect the real `care/` package (no `backend/app/` wrapper). Added entries for `llm/`, `services/`, `governance_check.py`, `roberta-large-ner-english`, and `layoutlm`. An LLM-citation artifact at the end of the file (`P\n::contentReference[oaicite:2]{index=2}\nDF`) was joined back to "PDF".
- 8 in-tree `CONTRACT` references repointed at `GOVERNANCE.md`.
- 4 `python -m care.cli ...` invocations rewritten to `care ...` (the actual console-script entry).
- README, `docs/redaction.md`, `docs/pii-policy.md` corrected to describe the real default PII chain (`regex` only) instead of an aspirational `regex → Presidio → ML` flow that was never the default.
- `docs/license-and-model-governance.md` provider table now lists every registered provider, including `roberta_ner`, `mock_pii`, `layoutlm`, `mock_vlm`, `hf_local`, and the cloud LLMs (`openai` / `anthropic` / `gemini`). A row for `openai_privacy_filter` was added in the same pass.
- Removed the EasyOCR + TrOCR-handwritten rows from the README OCR table — neither has a provider implementation; listing them was misleading.
- Stripped stale `(Phase N)` markers from `docs/architecture.md`, `docs/plugin-system.md`, and `care/cli/main.py` for shipped components.
- Fixed the CodeQL workflow header comment to match `.github/codeql/README.md` — the previous comment described a sanitizers-via-Models-as-Data setup that PR #16 removed because the QL `PathSanitizer` class hierarchy is not extensible via MaD.

### Lint gate added (`ruff`) + `pytest` config

This release wires `ruff` into the `governance.yml` CI workflow as a required step, with config in `pyproject.toml`. The lint sweep auto-fixed 507 issues across the codebase (mostly import sort + modern-syntax updates); 18 remaining issues — `B904` exception chaining, `F841` unused vars, `B905` `zip()` without `strict=`, an `E712 == True`, an `UP007 Optional[T]`, a `W291` trailing whitespace — were fixed manually. `[tool.pytest.ini_options]` is also now declared in `pyproject.toml` (was previously implicit). No mypy gate yet; that's a follow-up.

The visible result: PRs that introduce `ruff`-flagged issues now fail the policy-checks workflow at PR time. The bundled rule set (`E`, `F`, `W`, `I`, `B`, `UP`) is intentionally conservative — security-relevant only. We don't lint for stylistic preference.

### Dependency hygiene

The `[ml]` extra now requires `transformers>=5.6` (the floor for `OpenAIPrivacyFilterForTokenClassification`). Other floors bumped to recent stable releases: `torch>=2.5`, `openai>=1.55`, `anthropic>=0.50`. The Gemini provider now prefers the new `google-genai` SDK over the deprecated `google-generativeai` (auto-fallback if only the deprecated SDK is installed; no operator change required).

GitHub Actions versions are now current across the four workflows: `actions/checkout@v6`, `actions/setup-python@v6`, `actions/upload-artifact@v7`, `actions/download-artifact@v8`, `signpath/github-action-submit-signing-request@v2`. (The v4 → v7 upload-artifact bump also closed an artifact-name-spoofing class.)

### Build / repo hygiene

- `scripts/bench/data/` (synthetic-PII regression corpus + scored output) is now in `.gitignore` — it's a runtime harness artifact, not source. `models/**/.cache/` is correctly excluded by an existing `.cache` rule (audit verified).
- 10 stray `config.YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ.bak.yaml` files left over from local GUI Settings saves were deleted; they're already `.gitignore`d, but they were polluting `git status` for any contributor running the desktop wrapper locally.
- Stripped a stray tmux config line (`bind-key p display-message ...`) accidentally appended to four `.claude/rules/*.md` files. Local-only (`.claude/` is gitignored), but it was a copy-paste accident worth noting.

---

## Known limitations

### Code signing is still pending

SignPath enrolment for the project remains in progress. This release ships **unsigned** `.exe` and `.msi` artifacts. Verify each download against the **SHA-256 table at the bottom of this release page** before running.

The Windows build workflow degrades gracefully — when `vars.SIGNPATH_ORGANIZATION_ID` is empty, the sign job is skipped and the release job publishes the unsigned artifacts directly. Once enrolment lands, that variable will be populated and the next tag push will produce signed binaries on the same code path.

### What this provider does NOT do

Privacy Filter is a strong supplemental detector, not a single-layer redactor. Per OpenAI's model card, it should not be used as the only PII-detection layer for high-risk domains (medical, legal, financial) without human review. CARE already gates exports on `PII_UNMAPPED` and the QA review queue; keep `regex` in the chain alongside Privacy Filter. The Viterbi calibration that the upstream model uses for tunable precision/recall trade-offs is not yet wired into CARE — the standard HF `pipeline()` path runs untuned. A recall-first calibrated variant is a candidate follow-up.

---

## Asset verification

SHA-256 hashes for every artifact are listed at the bottom of this release page (auto-generated by the build workflow). Verify before running:

```powershell
Get-FileHash -Algorithm SHA256 .\CARE-0.2.0-core-online-Setup.exe
```

The hash should match the corresponding row exactly. If it doesn't, do not run the file — re-download or report the discrepancy.

---

## Upgrade notes

If you upgraded from v0.2.0-alpha.2:

- **No config migration required.** New optional fields under `pii.providers.openai_privacy_filter` have safe defaults — `enabled: false` — so an existing `config.yaml` continues to work unchanged.
- **No data migration required.** Per-job working trees and exports are untouched.
- **`ml` SKU users**: when you next install the `ml` flavour, `transformers` will resolve to `>=5.6`. Existing installs are unaffected; the floor applies to fresh `uv sync` only.

If you skipped v0.2.0-alpha.2 and are upgrading directly from v0.2.0-alpha, also read the v0.2.0-alpha.2 release notes (OnnxTR, mixed-PDF text-source routing, six bug fixes from the post-OnnxTR audit) — those changes are also part of this release.
